title: Grok Gets Automations: Scheduled and Email Triggered AI Jobs Arrive for Every User
desc: xAI adds Automations to Grok: describe a job once and it runs on a schedule or when an email lands. What agencies should route to prosumer agents vs n8n.
keywords: grok automations, grok scheduled tasks, grok email triggers, supergrok automations, ai assistant automations comparison
cta: Want automations that run your business, not just your inbox?
body:
July 20, 2026. xAI shipped Automations in Grok on July 16: jobs Grok runs on its own, described once in plain language, triggered by a schedule or an incoming email, with results reported back by email or app notification. The announcement puts the feature on grok.com and in the iOS and Android apps, and it completes a pattern: in under two weeks, every major AI assistant has shipped a personal automation layer.

What shipped

  1. Scheduled runs. Once, daily, weekdays, weekly, monthly, or yearly, at a chosen time in the user's timezone. A morning brief before the day starts or a rent reminder on the 1st are xAI's own examples.
  2. Email triggers. When an incoming email matches filters on sender, recipient, or subject, the automation fires with that email as context and Grok can respond to the actual message. Email triggers require SuperGrok; scheduled automations are available to everyone.
  3. Connectors and skills inside instructions. Typing @ mentions a connector so Grok uses it on every run, and automations can carry attached files and picked modes.
  4. Every run is a full conversation. Runs save to a history, each one can be opened, read, and continued where Grok left off, with notification by email, app, both, or neither.
  5. Creation from chat or templates. Asking Grok to check the news every morning and flag anything about pricing builds the automation directly, and a Run now button tests it immediately.

The automation layer is now table stakes

Claude Cowork brought scheduled background sessions to web and mobile on July 7, ChatGPT Work launched with scheduled tasks on July 10, and Grok followed on July 16. Independent comparisons, like this July 18 breakdown, already treat the three as a category. Grok's differentiator is the email trigger: reactive automation, not just scheduled automation, at consumer price points.

What it means for operators

Personal automations are genuinely useful for briefs, alerts, and inbox triage, and your clients are about to get used to them. That resets expectations: business owners who watch Grok summarize their inbox at 8am will ask why their CRM follow ups, quote requests, and fulfillment updates still wait for a human.

The honest answer is that prosumer automations run in one person's account with one person's context. They do not handle multi user state, approval chains, error handling, audit trails, or writes into systems of record with guardrails. That layer is where business grade AI automation lives, typically on n8n or custom pipelines wired into the CRM and fulfillment stack. The practical move for agencies: map which client requests belong in a $30 assistant subscription and which need real infrastructure, and be the firm that knows the difference. If the backlog of the second kind is growing, a dedicated n8n developer pays for itself quickly.
faqs: Automations are jobs Grok runs on its own, launched July 16, 2026. You describe the work once, choose a schedule or an email trigger, and Grok runs it and reports back by email or app notification. Every run is saved as a full conversation that can be reopened and continued.
Which Grok plan includes email triggered automations?

Scheduled automations are available to everyone on grok.com and the iOS and Android apps. Email triggers, where an automation fires when an incoming email matches filters on sender, recipient, or subject, are included with SuperGrok.
How do Grok Automations compare with ChatGPT tasks and Claude scheduled tasks?

All three now cover scheduled personal automation. Grok adds reactive email triggers at the consumer tier, Claude Cowork runs scheduled background sessions that work across files and connected tools, and ChatGPT Work bundles scheduled tasks into its workspace product. For individual briefs and alerts they are increasingly interchangeable.
When does a business need more than assistant automations?

When work involves more than one person's account: multi user state, approval flows, error handling, audit trails, or writing into a CRM or fulfillment system with guardrails. Those workflows belong on business automation platforms like n8n or custom pipelines, not inside a personal assistant subscription.
